---
title: "Hovmoller"
output: html_document
---

# Libraries

```{r}

# Libraries
library(terra)
library(tidyverse)

```

# Senegal

```{r}

# Collect files
ndvi_files <- list.files("Data/Senegal/Vegetation/NDVI", pattern = "\\.08\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE)

# Create an empty list to hold results
hov_data_list_senegal <- list()

for (file in ndvi_files) {
  # Extract the year from the filename (e.g., "NDVI.h16.2001.08.tif" -> "2001")
  year <- as.numeric(str_extract(basename(file), "\\d{4}"))
  
  # Load the raster
  r <- rast(file)
  
  # Convert the spatial map into a massive raw dataframe (X, Y, Value)
  df <- as.data.frame(r, xy = TRUE)
  colnames(df)[3] <- "NDVI"
  
  df$Lat_Deg <- df$y / 111319.5
  
  # Round the Y coordinate (Latitude) for clean horizontal "slices"
  # calculate the mean NDVI for every single slice.
  row_means <- df %>%
      mutate(Latitude_Slice = round(Lat_Deg, 1)) %>% 
      group_by(Latitude_Slice) %>%
      summarize(Mean_NDVI = mean(NDVI, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
      mutate(Year = year)
  
  # Save this year's slice data into list
  hov_data_list_senegal[[as.character(year)]] <- row_means
  print(paste("Processed Year:", year))
}

# Master dataframe
master_hov_df_senegal <- bind_rows(hov_data_list_senegal)

```

```{r}

start_year <- min(master_hov_df_senegal$Year)
end_year   <- max(master_hov_df_senegal$Year)

# Create diagram
p_hov_senegal <- ggplot(master_hov_df_senegal, aes(x = Year, y = Latitude_Slice)) +
  geom_raster(aes(fill = Mean_NDVI)) + 
  # Sand -> Dry Grass -> Jungle
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
    "#8c510a", # Brown (Desert)
    "#f6e8c3", # Cream (Dry Transition)
    "#78c679", # Light Green (Grassland)
    "#00441b"  # Dark Green (Lush)
  ),
  oob = scales::squish,
  limits = c(0, 0.8), # Locks the scale so colors don't shift
  name = "Mean NDVI"
  ) +
  geom_contour(aes(z = Mean_NDVI), breaks = 0.2, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_contour(aes(z = Mean_NDVI), breaks = 0.45, color = "darkblue",linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 2013, y = 19, label = "SAHARA DESERT", color = "white", fontface = "bold", alpha = 0.6, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 2013, y = 11, label = "LUSH VEGETATION", color = "white", fontface = "bold", alpha = 0.6, size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Sahara Expansion in Senegal (August 2000-2025)",
    subtitle = "Red Line = Desert Boundary (<0.2) | Blue Dashed = Lush Zone (>0.45)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Latitude (°N)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = seq(start_year, end_year, by = 1),
      labels = seq(start_year, end_year, by = 1), 
      expand = c(0, 0)
      ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

ggsave("Images/Diagrams/Hovmoller_Senegal_NDVI.png", plot = p_hov_senegal, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

p_hov_senegal

```

# Chad

```{r}

# Collect files
ndvi_files <- list.files("Data/Chad/Vegetation/NDVI", pattern = "\\.08\\.tif$", full.names = TRUE)

# Create an empty list to hold results
hov_data_list_chad <- list()

for (file in ndvi_files) {
  year <- as.numeric(str_extract(basename(file), "\\d{4}"))
  
  # Load the raster
  r <- rast(file)
  
  # Convert the spatial map into a massive raw dataframe (X, Y, Value)
  df <- as.data.frame(r, xy = TRUE)
  colnames(df)[3] <- "NDVI"
  
  df$Lat_Deg <- df$y / 111319.5
  
  # Round the Y coordinate (Latitude) for clean horizontal "slices"
  # calculate the mean NDVI for every single slice.
  row_means <- df %>%
      mutate(Latitude_Slice = round(Lat_Deg, 1)) %>% 
      group_by(Latitude_Slice) %>%
      summarize(Mean_NDVI = mean(NDVI, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
      mutate(Year = year)
  
  # Save this year's slice data into list
  hov_data_list_chad[[as.character(year)]] <- row_means
  print(paste("Processed Year:", year))
}

# Master dataframe
master_hov_df_chad <- bind_rows(hov_data_list_chad)

```

```{r}

start_year <- min(master_hov_df_chad$Year)
end_year   <- max(master_hov_df_chad$Year)

# Create diagram
p_hov_chad <- ggplot(master_hov_df_chad, aes(x = Year, y = Latitude_Slice)) +
  geom_raster(aes(fill = Mean_NDVI)) + 
  # Sand -> Dry Grass -> Jungle
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c(
    "#8c510a", # Brown (Desert)
    "#f6e8c3", # Cream (Dry Transition)
    "#78c679", # Light Green (Grassland)
    "#00441b"  # Dark Green (Lush)
  ),
  oob = scales::squish,
  limits = c(0, 0.8), # Locks the scale so colors don't shift
  name = "Mean NDVI"
  ) +
  geom_contour(aes(z = Mean_NDVI), breaks = 0.2, color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_contour(aes(z = Mean_NDVI), breaks = 0.45, color = "darkblue",linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 2013, y = 19, label = "SAHARA DESERT", color = "white", fontface = "bold", alpha = 0.6, size = 4) +
  annotate("text", x = 2013, y = 11, label = "LUSH VEGETATION", color = "white", fontface = "bold", alpha = 0.6, size = 4) +
  labs(
    title = "Sahara Expansion in Chad (August 2000-2025)",
    subtitle = "Red Line = Desert Boundary (<0.2) | Blue Dashed = Lush Zone (>0.45)",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Latitude (°N)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = seq(start_year, end_year, by = 1),
      labels = seq(start_year, end_year, by = 1), 
      expand = c(0, 0)
      ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

ggsave("Images/Diagrams/Hovmoller_Chad_NDVI.png", plot = p_hov_chad, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

p_hov_chad

```

# Combined Relative Expansion

```{r}

# Combine hovmoller data
senegal_final <- master_hov_df_senegal %>% mutate(Region = "Senegal")
chad_final    <- master_hov_df_chad %>% mutate(Region = "Chad")

combined_hov <- bind_rows(senegal_final, chad_final)

```

```{r}

# Calculate yearly desert edge (NDVI ~ 0.2)
edge_lats <- combined_hov %>%
  # We use a small range (0.18 to 0.22) because a perfect 0.2000 might not exist
  filter(Mean_NDVI >= 0.18 & Mean_NDVI <= 0.22) %>%
  group_by(Year, Region) %>%
  # Average the latitudes if multiple slices fall into that 0.18-0.22 range, 
  # then round it to 1 decimal place so it perfectly matches our 0.1 grid
  summarize(Edge_Lat = round(mean(Latitude_Slice, na.rm = TRUE), 1), .groups = "drop")

# Take a peek at the results to make sure it worked!
head(edge_lats)

```

```{r}

# Create relative scale
master_hov_relative <- combined_hov %>%
  # 1. Attach the zero-line (Edge_Lat) to the main dataset
  left_join(edge_lats, by = c("Year", "Region")) %>%
  
  # 2. The Shift: Calculate distance from the edge
  mutate(Relative_Lat = round(Latitude_Slice - Edge_Lat, 1)) %>%
  
  # 3. Crop the viewing window to 4 degrees North and South of the edge
  filter(Relative_Lat >= -6 & Relative_Lat <= 2) %>%
  
  # 4. Fill the gaps: Force R to create a row for every 0.1 slice in our window
  group_by(Region, Year) %>%
  complete(Relative_Lat = seq(-6, 2, by = 0.1)) %>%
  # Interpolate any missing NDVI values created by the shifting window
  mutate(Mean_NDVI = zoo::na.approx(Mean_NDVI, na.rm = FALSE)) %>%
  # Patch the extreme top/bottom edges just in case
  fill(Mean_NDVI, .direction = "downup") %>%
  ungroup()

# Check the new dataframe to ensure Relative_Lat goes from -4 to 4!
head(master_hov_relative)

```

```{r}

# Calculate lush edge (~0.45)
lush_edge <- master_hov_relative %>%
  filter(Mean_NDVI >= 0.70 & Mean_NDVI <= 1) %>%
  group_by(Year, Region) %>%
  summarize(Lush_Lat = mean(Relative_Lat, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

p_relative <- ggplot(master_hov_relative, aes(x = Year, y = Relative_Lat)) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = Mean_NDVI), width = 1, height = 0.1) +
  
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colors = c("#8c510a", "#f6e8c3", "#78c679", "#00441b"),
    oob = scales::squish,
    limits = c(0, 0.8),
    name = "Mean NDVI"
  ) +
  
  # The Desert Edge (Zero Line)
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linewidth = 1.2) +
  
  # The Lush Line (Using geom_smooth for a clean, flowing curve)
  geom_smooth(data = lush_edge, aes(x = Year, y = Lush_Lat), 
              method = "loess", span = 0.3, se = FALSE, 
              color = "blue", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  
  # On-plot Annotations
  annotate("text", x = 2013, y = 1.5, label = "SAHARA DESERT", color = "white", fontface = "bold", alpha = 0.8, size = 5) +
  annotate("text", x = 2013, y = -3.5, label = "LUSH VEGETATION", color = "white", fontface = "bold", alpha = 0.8, size = 5) +
  
  # Split into Chad and Senegal panels
  facet_wrap(~Region) +
  
  # Crop out the noisy top edge for Senegal
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-6, 2)) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Relative Sahara Expansion: Chad vs. Senegal",
    subtitle = "Red Line = Desert Edge | Blue Dashed = Lush Vegetation Boundary",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Distance from Desert Border (°)"
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2000, 2025, 5), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +
  
  # Upgraded text sizing for export readability
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

p_relative

```

```{r}

expansion_stats <- edge_lats %>%
  group_by(Region) %>%
  summarize(
    # The slope (coefficient of Year) tells us how many degrees it moved per year
    Degrees_Per_Year = coef(lm(Edge_Lat ~ Year))[2],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert the degrees to miles (1 deg = ~69 miles)
    # We use abs() to get the raw speed, since moving South means negative degrees
    Miles_Per_Year = abs(Degrees_Per_Year * 69)
  )

# Print the final stats!
print(expansion_stats)

```
